instructions.instruction: Use exponential backoff with full jitter for reconnect: delay = random in [0, min(maxMs, initialMs * multiplier^attempt))
instructions.original_snippets: function nextRetryDelay(attemptNumber, opts = {}) { const initialMs = opts.initialMs ?? 200; const maxMs = opts.maxMs ?? 30_000; const multiplier = opts.multiplier ?? 2; const expDelay = Math.min(maxMs, initialMs * Math.pow(multiplier, attemptNumber)); const jittered = Math.random() * expDelay; return jittered; }
instructions.relevant_when: Designing reconnect or per-call retry behavior for a PubNub client

instructions.instruction: Use 200ms initial, 30s cap, 2× multiplier, full jitter as the default backoff parameters
instructions.original_snippets: | `initialMs` | 200 | Fast recovery from a true blip | | `maxMs` | 30000 | Cap so we don't wait forever | | `multiplier` | 2 | Standard exponential growth | | jitter | full | Better than equal jitter for synchronization-breaking |
instructions.relevant_when: Picking concrete backoff numbers for a new client

instructions.instruction: Backoff must always include random jitter — pure exponential backoff still synchronizes across clients
instructions.original_snippets: **Backoff + jitter must always include random jitter.** A pure exponential backoff still synchronizes if every client computed the same delay from the same outage start time. ... Exponential backoff without jitter | Still synchronized — every client's 4th attempt fires at the same moment
instructions.relevant_when: Implementing any retry policy

instructions.instruction: Bound retries with a MAX_ATTEMPTS cap (~12 for ~5 min) and escalate to user / monitoring instead of looping forever
instructions.original_snippets: const MAX_ATTEMPTS = 12;     // ~5 min cumulative with capped backoff ... Don't retry forever. After a sensible cap, escalate to the user (show a "still trying to reconnect" UI) or surface to monitoring. ... Unbounded retries | Burns battery, fills logs, never tells the user
instructions.relevant_when: Building reconnect or retry logic

instructions.instruction: Reset the attempt counter ONLY on PNConnectedCategory / PNReconnectedCategory — never on a transient blip
instructions.original_snippets: Resetting `attempts = 0` on a network blip | Loses backoff state; resets the curve too eagerly. Reset only on `PNConnectedCategory` / `PNReconnectedCategory`.
instructions.relevant_when: Wiring a reconnect controller to status events

instructions.instruction: Drive reconnect from PubNub status events (PNNetworkDownCategory, PNNetworkIssuesCategory, PNTimeoutCategory schedule reconnect; PNConnected/PNReconnected reset)
instructions.original_snippets: pubnub.addListener({ status: (s) => { switch (s.category) { case 'PNConnectedCategory': case 'PNReconnectedCategory': reconnectController.reset(); ... case 'PNNetworkDownCategory': case 'PNNetworkIssuesCategory': case 'PNTimeoutCategory': reconnectController.scheduleReconnect( ... ); break; } } });
instructions.relevant_when: Listening for PubNub status events to drive reconnect

instructions.instruction: Generate the message_id ONCE at message creation time, never inside the retry loop — same id flows through every retry
instructions.original_snippets: Generate the ID **once** at the moment of message creation (not at the moment of send). The same ID flows through every retry attempt. ... Generating a new UUID inside `publishWithRetry` for each attempt | Generate once outside the retry loop
instructions.relevant_when: Wrapping pubnub.publish in a retry loop

instructions.instruction: Use crypto.randomUUID() (UUID v4) as the message_id source for client-generated publish identity
instructions.original_snippets: | `crypto.randomUUID()` v4 | Standard, collision-free, 16 bytes encoded as 36 chars | A bit long | ... For most use cases: **UUID v4 via `crypto.randomUUID()`**.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating a stable message identifier

instructions.instruction: Receivers dedup by message_id using a Set or bounded LRU; never assume PubNub dedups for you
instructions.original_snippets: const seenIds = new LRU({ max: 10_000 }); ... if (id && seenIds.has(id)) return; // duplicate; drop ... Treating PubNub as if it dedupes | PubNub does not dedup; it's the consumer's job
instructions.relevant_when: Subscribing to a channel where retries or live+history overlap can produce duplicates

instructions.instruction: Prefer message_id over timetoken as the dedup key — message_id survives retries and history fetches
instructions.original_snippets: Always prefer `message_id` when the producer is under your control. ... | `message_id` (client-generated) | Producer is idempotent | Best — survives retries, history fetches, anything | | `timetoken` | Producer is not idempotent but you only care about this connection | Unique per publish, but a retry creates a new timetoken |
instructions.relevant_when: Choosing the dedup key for live+history merge

instructions.instruction: For live + history merge, process the catch-up history first, then subscribe live — order matters so the dedup set is pre-populated before live messages arrive
instructions.original_snippets: Order matters: process caught-up messages first (they're older), then start consuming live. The dedup `Set` already contains everything from the catch-up, so any live message that overlaps is dropped. ... Dedup the message before the catch-up pass populates the set | Process catch-up first, live second
instructions.relevant_when: Implementing offline catch-up + live subscribe

instructions.instruction: Use a bounded LRU (e.g., 10000 entries) for the dedup set in a long-running session, not an unbounded Set
instructions.original_snippets: For longer sessions, cap memory with an LRU ... 10,000 entries covers most chat / consumer apps. ... Unbounded in-memory `Set` for a long-running process | Use LRU or TTL
instructions.relevant_when: Building dedup state for a long-running client

instructions.instruction: Default to per-channel dedup state (one LRU per channel), not a single global set across all channels
instructions.original_snippets: Default to per-channel. ... One global set across all channels and users | Per-channel (or per-(channel, user)) instead
instructions.relevant_when: Designing dedup state for an app that subscribes to multiple channels

instructions.instruction: Offline queue must be PERSISTENT (localStorage / IndexedDB / SQLite) — never in-memory only
instructions.original_snippets: **Offline queue must be persistent** (localStorage / SQLite / IndexedDB), not in-memory. ... In-memory queue | Lost on app crash; persist
instructions.relevant_when: Building an offline publish queue for a mobile or web client

instructions.instruction: Offline queue must be FIFO-ordered, idempotent (each item carries its message_id), and bounded by count and age
instructions.original_snippets: | **Ordered** | FIFO drain in enqueue order | | **Idempotent** | Each queued item carries a stable `message_id` from creation time | | **Bounded** | Cap by count + age; surface to user when at the cap |
instructions.relevant_when: Designing an offline publish queue

instructions.instruction: On reaching MAX_ATTEMPTS for a queued item, move it to a dead-letter store — never silently auto-discard
instructions.original_snippets: if (item.attempts > MAX_ATTEMPTS) { await db.delete('outbox', item.id); await db.put('dead-letter', item); ... Auto-discarding dead letters | Move to a dead-letter store and surface
instructions.relevant_when: Handling persistent retry failures in the offline queue

instructions.instruction: Trigger queue drain on app start, on PNReconnectedCategory / PNConnectedCategory status events, on a 30s periodic backstop, and on each enqueue (if online)
instructions.original_snippets: Drain on: - App start - Reconnect (`PNReconnectedCategory`) ... - A periodic interval (every 30s) as a backstop - Each `enqueue` (try to drain immediately if online)
instructions.relevant_when: Wiring a queue worker into the lifecycle of a PubNub client

instructions.instruction: Every message envelope MUST include schema_version (integer), message_id (UUID), sent_at (ms), type (discriminator), and payload
instructions.original_snippets: | `schema_version` | Integer. Bumped on any change to the shape. | | `message_id` | UUID v4 for idempotent publish + dedup. | | `sent_at` | Producer timestamp in ms. Useful for stale-message detection. | | `type` | Discriminator if the payload shape varies by event type. | | `payload` | The application-specific body. |
instructions.relevant_when: Building a message envelope for publish

instructions.instruction: Schema version is an immutable monotonic integer; never reuse a previous version number for a different shape
instructions.original_snippets: **Schema version is immutable per message.** Never reuse a version number for a different shape. ... When you bump, **never reuse the previous version number for a different shape**. v1 is forever v1. ... Reusing v1 with a different shape | v1 is forever v1; bump to v2
instructions.relevant_when: Versioning message envelopes across releases

instructions.instruction: Receivers are tolerant readers: log-and-drop messages below MIN_SUPPORTED, log-and-drop messages above MAX_SUPPORTED (and prompt update), never crash on unknown fields
instructions.original_snippets: 1. Read `schema_version` first. 2. If version is in a known list, parse with the corresponding handler. 3. If version is below the minimum supported, drop with a log line. 4. If version is above the maximum supported, drop with a log line and suggest an app update. 5. Never crash on unknown fields
instructions.relevant_when: Implementing a receiver that consumes versioned envelopes

instructions.instruction: Migration flow: at T1 receivers handle BOTH old and new versions; at T2 producer migrates to new version; at T3 drop old handler — never go T0 → T2 directly
instructions.original_snippets: T0: Producer + receiver both at v1. T1: Receiver updated to handle BOTH v1 and v2 ... T2: Producer updated to write v2. T3: After confidence period, drop v1 handler in receivers. Never go T0 → T2 directly without T1.
instructions.relevant_when: Planning a schema version bump rollout
